experiences.intentions: The active I piece is horizontal near the top, spanning columns 3-6 with an empty board below. Early on, the goal is to place pieces flat and low to preserve flexibility; with next pieces O, L, Z, S, keeping the stack even is best. Candidate moves: rotating vertical is unnecessary on an empty board and creates a tall column; moving left or right before dropping has no immediate advantage because the surface is empty and central placement is fine. soft_drop only advances one row, while hard_drop efficiently places the piece on the bottom as a flat 4-wide foundation. Therefore hard_drop best advances the goal.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for a fixed O near the top and the active I piece lying horizontally on the bottom row with open space all around. Immediate goal is to place the I cleanly to set up future pieces without wasting moves. Since the piece is already at the bottom, soft drop and hard drop would just lock it where it is; lateral movement can improve placement first. Moving left shifts the I toward the wall, keeping the center more open for upcoming L, Z, S, T pieces and creates a flatter, more flexible stack than locking it in the middle. Rotations are unnecessary because only the horizontal orientation is valid here. Therefore the best action now is left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Board is almost empty with an O near the top and an I lying horizontally on the bottom row spanning columns 3-6. The active piece appears to be the O near the top; rotating it changes nothing, and dropping it now would settle it safely on top of the I at the center. Goal is to place the current piece efficiently and set up future L/Z/S/T pieces while avoiding unnecessary delay. Candidate actions: left/right could reposition the O for side wells, but with only one move now and no urgent threat, the strongest immediate progress is to lock it in. rotate actions are useless for O, soft_drop is slower than necessary, no_op wastes tempo. Hard dropping advances the game state cleanly.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top in spawn orientation. Stack is flat and low: O at columns 3-4 above an I spanning columns 3-6, leaving a clean left side and open well elsewhere. Goal is to place the L efficiently to keep the surface flat and prepare for upcoming Z,S,T,J. A strong immediate plan is to move the L toward the left so it can later drop into the open area without creating overhangs over the central stack. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place it high on the center stack and create unevenness; soft_drop commits vertically too early; rotations are not yet necessary because horizontal movement is still available and flexibility is highest. Moving left advances toward the open left side and preserves options.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active L piece is high near the center-left, while the stack below is low: an O block sits over columns 3-4 and an I bar fills the bottom row columns 3-6, leaving a flat platform but no immediate line clear. Goal is to place the L efficiently without creating holes and preferably use the left-side shelf above the O. From the spawn orientation, moving left positions the L better toward columns 2-4, where it can later drop to rest neatly on/next to the O stack. Rotating now is less urgent than lateral alignment, and hard/soft drop from current x would commit to a less useful central placement.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is mostly empty except for a flat I piece on the bottom row and an O block stacked above its left-center, creating a broad open field. The active L is high near the top in its spawn orientation. My goal now is to place pieces efficiently to keep the surface flat and avoid creating awkward overhangs; since the stack is very low, immediate survival is not a concern. A hard drop in the current orientation would place the L horizontally on top of the O/left side, making a reasonable low stack, while moving or rotating first is not clearly better without more setup and would just delay placement. So the best immediate action is to lock in the L with a hard drop.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top, with a flat stack at the bottom: I on the floor, O above center-right, and L creating a left-side notch. Immediate threat is low since the board is mostly empty. Goal now is efficient placement to build a flat surface and avoid awkward overhangs before upcoming S,T,J pieces. Candidate actions: hard_drop would place the current horizontal Z onto the existing stack, likely creating a manageable contour without wasting turns; moving/rotating could optimize further, but with a very open board and no pressure, the simplest strong play is to lock it down promptly. Soft drop is slower, and no_op wastes time. Hard drop best advances the sub-goal of consolidating the low stack.

Lateral movement or rotation could refine placement, but with only one action this turn, the strongest progress toward locking a useful piece is to drop it. Soft drop advances less; rotations are not clearly superior given the open board. Therefore hard_drop is the best action.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the upper-left/center, currently horizontal with cells around rows 6-8. The stack below is uneven but there is open space; no immediate top-out threat. Goal is to place this Z efficiently without creating awkward holes, ideally using the left-side contour above the J/L area. Rotating right makes the Z vertical, which should fit more naturally into the available left-center slope and improves future placement options for upcoming L,S,J,T. Other moves like hard/soft drop now would commit the current horizontal shape in a less flexible way, and lateral moves without rotating don’t yet exploit the contour.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is a Z near the upper-left/mid-left, currently horizontal with occupied cells around rows 8-10. Rotation right is blocked, so useful options are lateral movement or dropping. The surface below has a notch around the left-center: row 15 is 'J...ZZ....', row 16 'JJJ.LZZ...', row 17 '..LLL.....', which creates a vertical channel near columns 3-4 that suits the current horizontal Z better if shifted left. Goal is to place the Z cleanly to reduce holes and fit the left-center contour before the stack rises. Moving left positions the Z over that notch; moving right would head toward the T/S stack and likely create worse overhangs, while dropping now would commit to a less aligned placement.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top in horizontal orientation. The stack is low and uneven, with useful cavities around the center-left and center-right, but there is no immediate threat since the piece is very high and board danger is low. Goal is to place the L to improve the surface for upcoming S, J, T, O pieces rather than drop it prematurely in a poor orientation. A good sub-goal is to move it toward the left-side notch above the existing L/J structure around rows 15-17, where an L can fit well after further movement. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop commit too early while still far from target; rotations are not yet necessary because horizontal L is already flexible and can be rotated later near the destination; right moves away from the more promising left-side placement. Therefore the best immediate action is to shift left.

experiences.intentions: Active L piece is high and unobstructed, currently in vertical-right orientation at center-right. The stack below has a mild cavity around columns 4-5 above the lower left/middle, and there is plenty of space to maneuver before locking. Immediate goal is to place this L to improve surface flatness and avoid creating overhangs. A good sub-goal is to move toward the left-middle where the L can likely tuck near the existing L/OO structure on later turns; dropping now at current column would create a tall isolated bump over the central area. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop commit too early in a mediocre central position; rotations are not urgent since current orientation is already flexible; right worsens the future placement. Moving left starts positioning toward the more useful landing zone while preserving options for later rotation/drop.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is an L in rotation 1, high above the stack with no immediate collision pressure. Surface analysis: there is a left-side notch around rows 16-17 and some unevenness, while the center-right is relatively cluttered. Goal is to place the L to improve the lower stack without creating overhangs; first sub-goal is to move it toward the useful left-side cavity before committing to a drop. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place it near the current column and likely waste the piece high on the stack; soft_drop reduces flexibility too early; rotations are possible but not yet necessary because current vertical-L can still be shifted and later rotated as needed. Moving left starts aligning the piece with the left-side gap and keeps all future options open better than right or no_op.

experiences.intentions: The active L piece is high above the stack around columns 2-4. The surface below has a notch around column 4 above the I/T area, and there is still plenty of vertical space, so immediate hard drop is premature. My goal is to place the L efficiently to reduce holes and set up upcoming S,J,T,O pieces. Among candidate moves, shifting left would better align the L with the left-side contour and available cavity than moving right, while rotating now is not yet necessary because the current horizontal L can still be adjusted after further descent. Soft drop only commits downward without improving alignment. Therefore the best immediate sub-goal is to start moving the piece left into a more useful lane.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is an L near the upper-left of the stack. The surface below has a left-side notch around columns 1-2 above the existing J/Z structure, while the right side is more uneven and risks creating overhangs. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would lock the current orientation in a mediocre spot; rotations 2 or 3 could fit the left contour better, but first we need to shift left before dropping. Moving right worsens placement. Soft drop commits downward too early. Best immediate sub-goal is to align toward the left notch, so move left.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S near the upper-left/mid board with only two rotations. Surface below has a notch around columns 2-4 above the existing stack; moving/locking soon is better than stalling. Goal is to place S cleanly to reduce holes and set up upcoming J/T/O pieces. Candidate actions: rotate would switch to horizontal but current vertical S already looks aligned to slide into the left-side contour; left shifts toward the available pocket, right moves away, drops commit immediately without first improving alignment. Best immediate sub-goal is to move left one step to position for a cleaner descent on the next turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S near the upper-left, currently in vertical orientation with open space below/around it. The stack is low and uneven but not threatening yet; immediate opportunity is to place the S efficiently on the left without creating extra overhangs. Goal now is to advance the S downward into available left-side contour while preserving central flexibility for upcoming J,T,O,O. Rotating would switch to horizontal and may fit differently, but from the shown board the current vertical S can likely settle cleanly by dropping; lateral moves are less urgent than gaining vertical progress. hard_drop risks committing without full certainty of best placement, so a soft_drop is the safest progress action.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near the center with open air below. The stack is low and uneven, with a noticeable left-side structure and no immediate threat, so the goal is to place J efficiently to improve the surface rather than rush. A good sub-goal is to move the J toward the left to fit future contours while we still have plenty of height. Among candidates, left starts shifting the piece toward useful left-center placements; right moves away from the main uneven area, rotations are premature before horizontal positioning, and dropping now would commit too early from a very high position.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high near the center-left in horizontal orientation. The stack below has a relatively flat top around rows 9-11 with some cavities, especially on the left/mid-left; there is no immediate danger since the piece is near the top with ample space. Goal is to place J to improve the left-side surface and avoid creating overhangs; the quickest progress this turn is lateral positioning before dropping. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place the J awkwardly near current x and likely leave a jagged surface; soft_drop commits downward before aligning; rotations are not yet necessary because the current horizontal J can fit useful notches after shifting. Moving left starts steering toward the left-side structure and keeps options open for later drop/rotation.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is near the upper-left with plenty of air below. The stack is uneven but not threatening yet; a useful opportunity is to place J to support the left side and avoid creating awkward overhangs. Immediate goal is to improve placement flexibility before committing to a drop. Rotating right changes J from flat-top orientation into a vertical shape that can fit more cleanly along the left contours and gives better options on subsequent moves than moving or dropping immediately.

experiences.intentions: Current active J is high and unobstructed. Surface below has a left-side bump and central wells; immediate threat is low since stack is mid-height. Goal is to place this J to improve the surface for upcoming T,O,O,J pieces, ideally filling left/center unevenness rather than committing to a premature drop in a mediocre orientation. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would lock the current vertical J without horizontal adjustment, likely creating awkward overhangs; soft_drop also commits downward before positioning. Rotating to horizontal could be useful, but first shifting left gives more placement flexibility toward the left-side terrain where J often fits well. Right moves away from the useful left structure. Best immediate sub-goal is to translate left while still high, preserving future rotate/drop options.

experiences.intentions: Active T is near the top with empty space below; the stack is low and there is no immediate threat. The surface around columns 4-6 has a slight central feature, and with next pieces O,O,J,Z it is useful to place this T efficiently without creating overhangs. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would place the T flat quickly but without aligning to a specific notch; rotating could prepare a tuck but there is no urgent need and no clear immediate T-slot under the spawn. Sliding left/right first may improve placement later, but with only one action now the safest progress toward settling the piece is to descend. Soft_drop advances toward a controlled placement while preserving future lateral/rotation options.

experiences.intentions: Active T is near the top with open space below; the stack is uneven but not in immediate danger. Goal is to place the T efficiently without creating overhangs, and ideally keep options open for upcoming O,O,J,Z. A lateral move now helps align the T over the center-left contour better than dropping immediately; rotation isn't urgent because current spawn orientation is flexible and there is time. Moving left starts positioning the T toward a cleaner landing zone and preserves future rotation/drop choices.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high and centered. Surface below is uneven, with a useful notch around columns 4-6 in the upper-middle, but immediate hard drop from current orientation would likely create a bump. Rotating right to orientation 1 makes the T vertical with its stem downward, which better prepares to slot into a notch on subsequent moves and preserves flexibility; moving sideways or dropping now commits too early from a high spawn position. Goal is to place the T cleanly without worsening the stack, so the best immediate sub-goal is orienting it for fitting opportunities.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is a T near the upper-left/mid-left, currently in rotation 1. The stack is still low with no immediate danger, so the priority is improving placement flexibility rather than rushing a drop. Directly hard/soft dropping now would commit the T high on the left where it doesn't seem to fill a clean cavity. Moving right may help later, but first rotating to a flatter orientation gives better options to slot the T into surface notches as it descends. From the shown states, rotating right to rotation 2 removes the left-side overhang and creates a standard horizontal T shape that is generally easier to place cleanly. So the best immediate sub-goal is to reorient the T for a stronger future placement.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

The stack is low and there are no immediate threats, so the goal is to place the T cleanly to avoid creating holes and set up future O/O/J/Z pieces. So the best single-step action is to rotate clockwise.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is O at the top center already in its only orientation. The stack below has a pronounced left-side cavity around columns 0-2 in the upper half, while the center-right is relatively open. Since O cannot rotate meaningfully, the actionable choices are horizontal movement or dropping. Immediate goal is to place the O cleanly without creating overhangs, and moving left helps target the left depression where a 2x2 block can cap space efficiently. A single left step advances toward that placement while preserving flexibility for later moves; hard/soft drop now would lock too early near center, and right moves away from the useful cavity.

Goal is to place the O efficiently and start moving it toward a supportive flat spot; a single left step begins aligning it with the best nearby landing zone. Candidates: rotate/no_op do nothing valuable, right moves away from the left shelf, soft/hard drop commits too early. Best action: left.

Since the piece spawns high and there are no immediate threats at the top, the sub-goal is to move it toward a useful landing zone before committing. Moving left starts aligning the O toward the central-left openings; hard dropping now would just stack high in the center, and rotation/no_op do nothing useful.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is O at the top near columns 3-4, with no alternate rotations. The stack is uneven but there is an obvious 2x2 cavity around rows 3-4 and cols 4-5 directly beneath the current piece, perfectly fitting the O. Goal is to place the O cleanly to fill that notch and keep the surface compact. left/right would misalign from the cavity, rotation does nothing, and no_op wastes time. soft_drop moves toward the target but hard_drop safely locks the O exactly into the 2x2 gap immediately.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

Immediate goal is to place it efficiently without creating holes while preparing for upcoming Z/L/T/I. A rotation now is low-risk because there is ample space and preserves horizontal flexibility afterward. Candidate moves left/right just shift without improving fit yet; dropping in current orientation is premature. So rotate clockwise to set up a cleaner placement.
